assertions.description: The post-triage summary comment (Step 7) includes an @mention of the vulnerability issue's reporter using an ADF mention node with account ID '557058:psirt-analyst-mock-id'
assertions.evidence: In remediation.md (Step 7), the ADF JSON contains a mention node: { "type": "mention", "attrs": { "id": "557058:psirt-analyst-mock-id", "text": "@psirt-analyst" } }. The account ID matches exactly.

assertions.description: The reporter @mention in Step 7 is present by default without any ProdSec configuration — it uses the reporter field from the Jira issue
assertions.evidence: In data-extraction.md (Step 1), the reporter is extracted from the Jira issue: 'Reporter | psirt-analyst (account ID: 557058:psirt-analyst-mock-id)'. In remediation.md, the @mention Details section states: 'The reporter of TC-8001 is psirt-analyst with Jira account ID 557058:psirt-analyst-mock-id' and 'This @mention is mandatory per the SKILL.md -- the reporter field is always available on the Jira issue.' This confirms it uses the reporter field from the Jira issue, not ProdSec configuration.

assertions.description: The Affects Versions correction comment (Step 3) includes an @mention of the ProdSec contact using an ADF mention node with account ID '557058:prodsec-mock-account-id'
assertions.evidence: In affects-versions.md (Step 3), the ADF JSON contains a mention node: { "type": "mention", "attrs": { "id": "557058:prodsec-mock-account-id", "text": "@ProdSec" } }. The account ID matches exactly.

assertions.description: The ProdSec @mention appears before the Comment Footnote in the Affects Versions correction comment
assertions.evidence: In affects-versions.md, the ProdSec mention node appears at lines 44-51 in the ADF content array. The Comment Footnote (rule + AI-generated attribution paragraph) appears at lines 53-80. The mention precedes the footnote in document order.

assertions.description: Step 0 extracts the ProdSec Jira account ID from the Security Configuration as an optional field without raising an error
assertions.evidence: While no explicit Step 0 output file exists, the ProdSec account ID '557058:prodsec-mock-account-id' was successfully extracted and used in Step 3 without any error. In affects-versions.md, the @mention Details section states: 'ProdSec @mention: Included in the comment because ProdSec Jira account ID is configured (557058:prodsec-mock-account-id)'. This confirms the ID was read from Security Configuration as an available (optional) field without raising an error.
